memory:memory/2026-04-20.md:21:26.snippet: - Best customers value value-for-money, high-end experience, smooth delivery, prestige, networking, socialising, and social media shareability. - Differentiation: unique all-day premium urban event combining sport, lifestyle, music, fashion, hospitality, and prestige. Competes with ASB Classic, rugby, and other sporting options. - Market differences: Christchurch buyers purchase earlier; Auckland ticket buyers are later and weather-sensitive. Debbie explicitly asked for this information to be saved to long-term memory.

memory:memory/2026-04-20.md:11:17.snippet: - Sponsorship is sold year-round, with a mix of annual and multi-year deals. Private marquees sell as soon as dates launch. VIP and GA launch together after sponsorship and marquees. Biggest cash flow squeeze is immediately after events when supplier invoices land. - Hardest sales: team sponsorships. Most predictable in NZ: marquee sales. - Long-term ambition: be the number 1 sporting and lifestyle event in NZ and Australia, premium, iconic, culturally relevant, exclusive, globally recognised, selling out in the first week. - Growth goals include more Australian cities, possible USA cities, doubled sponsorship revenue, multi-day events in some markets, stronger recurring sponsorship, repeata

memory:memory/2026-04-20.md:28:40.snippet: - Debbie chooses quality over speed and usually asks Simon to clarify priorities when tensions appear. - Good growth means profitable events, not growth for appearance. Healthy growth signs include strong sponsorship sales, about 600 marquee attendees in year one, over 600 VIP attendees in year one, about 600 GA attendees in year one, and strong website demand for marquee and sponsorship information. - Personal success for Debbie means automating as many processes as possible with Monday and the assistant, getting marquee customers all key information at least 2 months before the event, and keeping supplier budgets under control. - Debbie wants to become more sales-focused, systems-focused,

memory:memory/2026-04-20.md:1:13.snippet: # 2026-04-20 ## Canonical business context interview with Debbie Debbie asked for an in-depth canonical interview to serve as the source of truth for future strategic and business-aligned work. Key context captured: - Debbie is Operations Manager for the polo events business. She handles marquee sales, customer liaison, ticketing admin, invoicing, sponsorship admin, and supplier coordination. - Business operates established NZ events in Christchurch and Auckland, newer Australian events in Sydney and Portsea, with Brisbane planned and Melbourne possible. Singapore is on hold while they try to secure ponies. - Core team: Simon Wilson (MD), Debbie, Francesca Morelli (marketing and ticketing)
